summary: Matched several Fox/Falco/Kirby blaster item functions in src/melee/it/items/itfoxblaster.c. The PR cleaned up decompiler scaffolding, corrected callback return types to bool, changed efSync_Spawn to return int and used that return value for the blaster effect handle/state field, tightened item-var types to bool, adjusted data symbol boundaries, and made small matching-oriented expression/stack-shape changes. The decomp.dev report credited 5 new itfoxblaster matches and +2544 matched bytes.
what_changed: In itfoxblaster.c, item callback prototypes and definitions that returned 0/1 were changed from int to bool, including it_802AEBCC, it_802AEF08, it_802AEF10, it_802AEFBC, it_802AF064, and it_802AF17C.

efSync_Spawn's declaration in efsync.h was changed from void to int, and it_802ADF10 now assigns the return value of efSync_Spawn(1166/1196, ...) into item->xDD4_itemVar.foxblaster.xDE4 instead of spawning then forcing xDE4 to 0.
itCharItems.h changed foxblaster xDE0 and gfx_spawn_var from s32 flags to bool, with corresponding call sites changed from 0/1 tests and assignments to false/true and direct boolean checks.
GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ-style access replaced repeated raw item_gobj->user_data and cast-heavy code in several places, while unnecessary explicit casts and commented-out m2c scaffolding were removed.
The blaster spawn helpers now use literal 0.0F instead of the removed static zero float it_804DCF38 for prev_pos.z and velocity initialization.
it_802AE63C gained PAD_STACK(8), preserving stack layout needed for a match.
The state index tables it_803F6E3C and it_803F6E68 were moved later in itfoxblaster.c; it_803F6E68 was changed from a 12-element s32 table with an extra trailing 0 to an 11-element int table.
config/GALE01/symbols.txt adjusted it_803F6CA8's size from 0x180 to 0x158 and exposed lbl_803F6E00 as a 0x25 data:string symbol.
ftCo_ItemThrow.c split the sign-extension expression for int_angle into two statements, preserving the same left shift then arithmetic right shift behavior with a compiler-friendlier shape.
The decomp.dev bot reported 5 new matches in main/melee/it/items/itfoxblaster: it_802AF064, it_802AE994, it_802AE63C, it_802AEBCC, and it_802AE200.
smart_moves: Treating efSync_Spawn as returning int allowed the effect-spawn return value to become part of the reconstructed logic, explaining xDE4 better than a separate zero assignment and improving matchability.
Converting simple item-state flags from s32 to bool simplified control flow and made return values/flag use match the apparent ABI semantics of item callbacks.

Moving static data near the functions that use it, and correcting symbol sizes in symbols.txt, aligned source-level declarations with the binary's data boundaries, including separating out lbl_803F6E00 as a string.
Using GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ and removing redundant casts reduced noisy decompiler artifacts without changing behavior, making the remaining code easier to match and review.
Adding a small PAD_STACK only where needed in it_802AE63C shows a targeted stack-shape tactic rather than broad source churn.
Rewriting the msid filtering in it_802AEBCC as a switch over the skipped states removed a goto-shaped reconstruction while still matching.
Introducing copy_jobj_scale isolated the scale-copy pattern for it_802AF064 and produced the desired code shape around HSD_JObjGetScaleY and HSD_JObjSetScale.
decomp_lessons: For item callbacks, verify whether 0/1 returns are semantically bool; changing signatures and documentation from int to bool can be both clearer and match-improving.
Effect-spawn APIs may return handles or identifiers even when older declarations say void; if a field is immediately zeroed near an effect spawn, check whether the original stored the spawn return.
Boolean item variables should be modeled as bool when they are only used as flags; this can improve comparisons, assignments, and readability.
Data layout fixes may require editing both C declaration placement and symbols.txt sizes; an oversized object in symbols.txt can hide a following string/data symbol.
Small expression-shape changes, such as splitting a sign-extension cast/shift into separate assignments, can matter for matching without changing semantics.
Use PAD_STACK sparingly and locally when a function is close to matching but stack allocation differs.
Replacing raw user_data access with project macros like GET_ITEM can reduce cast noise, but preserve repeated reloads when the compiler needs them for the match.
naming_conventions: Unidentified item functions retained address-based names such as it_802ADF10, it_802AEBCC, it_802AF064, and it_802AE994.
Unidentified data symbols retained address-based names such as it_803F6CA8, it_803F6E28, it_803F6E3C, and it_803F6E68.
A newly split data string was named lbl_803F6E00 in symbols.txt rather than being folded into the preceding table symbol.
Foxblaster item variable fields remain offset-style names such as xDD4, xDD8, xDE0, xDE4, xDFC, xE14, and gfx_spawn_var, with comments documenting observed behavior.
Helper naming used descriptive local names where possible, e.g. jobj_child and copy_jobj_scale, while preserving unknown global symbol names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed efSync_Spawn from void to int and assigned its return directly to xDE4 to match original register/value flow.
Inserted PAD_STACK(8) in it_802AE63C to force the expected stack frame.
Split `((int) (s16_var1 << 20)) >> 20` into `int_angle = s16_var1 << 20; int_angle = int_angle >> 20;` in ftCo_ItemThrow.c for matching code generation.
Replaced a static zero global, it_804DCF38, with literal 0.0F assignments, removing an unnecessary data reference.
Adjusted table sizes/order so it_803F6CA8 no longer swallowed following data and lbl_803F6E00 could be represented separately.
Used bool returns and false/true constants to steer callback epilogues and comparisons toward the matched binary form.
Removed large commented-out alternate reconstructions and #if 0 scaffolding after achieving cleaner matching source.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 32.06% (+0.07%, +2544 bytes) and listed 5 new itfoxblaster matches: it_802AF064, it_802AE994, it_802AE63C, it_802AEBCC, and it_802AE200.
follow_up_queries: Check other callers of efSync_Spawn to see whether the int return type reveals more effect handle fields or matching opportunities.
Search item callback tables for int-returning functions that only return 0/1 and may need bool signatures.
Audit nearby item data symbols around it_803F6CA8 through it_803F6E68 for additional table/string boundary corrections.
Look for other decompiler sign-extension expressions in fighter code that may match better when split into separate shift statements.
Compare Fox/Falco blaster and Kirby blaster item logic for opportunities to name xDE4, xDE0, gfx_spawn_var, and related foxblaster fields more semantically.
